story.iWant: to create Flyway database migrations for Phase 2 schema changes
story.soThat: the database schema supports thread branching, project collaboration, and insights features with full audit trail support

tasks.task: Create consolidated V2.1 migration for all Phase 2 tables with full audit support (desktop_threads, desktop_messages, projects, project_members, saved_reports)
Verify schema alignment with BaseEntity audit pattern and Moku/Holo standards

architectureDecisions.decision: All desktop tables now align with Moku/Holo audit standards using dual ownership tracking: created_user_id (UUID business ownership) and created_by/last_modified_by (VARCHAR system audit)
Consolidated single V2.1 migration instead of separate V2.1-V2.5 migrations for simpler deployment and atomic schema updates
Renamed created_by UUID field to created_user_id for clarity, added created_by VARCHAR for Spring Security system audit
Desktop tables (desktop_threads, desktop_messages) use client-generated UUIDs for offline-first architecture

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: V2.1 migration creates all Phase 2 tables with full audit support (desktop_threads, desktop_messages, projects, project_members, saved_reports)
All tables include audit columns: created_user_id (UUID business ownership), created_by/last_modified_by (VARCHAR system audit)
All tables follow Moku/Holo audit standards (extend BaseEntity pattern, @Audited for Hibernate Envers)
Schema includes proper timestamps (created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) with auto-update triggers
All CHECK constraints enforce valid enum values (status, type, role, branch_type)
All foreign key constraints correctly cascade deletes or set NULL as specified
All indexes exist on foreign key columns and frequently queried fields (type, status, updated_at, project_id, user_id)
Unique indexes prevent duplicate memberships and idempotent message IDs

doc.title: Database Schema Specification
doc.snippet: Complete database schema for desktop_threads, desktop_messages, projects, project_members, workflows, workflow_executions, saved_reports tables with all columns, constraints, indexes, and views.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Detailed entity definitions with SQL DDL, CHECK constraints, foreign key behavior, and JSONB column specifications for all Phase 2 database changes.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Database Migration Sequence with 10 detailed steps covering creation, testing, rollback scripts, and version control commit process.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Backend dependencies: PostgreSQL Driver 42.7+, Flyway 10.x, Testcontainers 1.19+ for migration testing, JUnit 5.10+ for test framework.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Migration performance: Each Flyway migration must complete in < 5 minutes on databases with up to 100K threads. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid table locks > 30 seconds.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: RISK-1 (Medium): Database migration may exceed 5-minute target on production. Mitigation: Test on production-sized dataset clone, use concurrent indexes. RISK-7 (Medium): Rollback scripts may not handle data transformations correctly. Mitigation: Test rollback with realistic data, document data loss scenarios.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Moku API: Spring Boot REST service responsible for Users, orgs, projects, threads, workflows, insights. Backend technology stack: Spring Boot REST + PostgreSQL 14+.

doc.title: Epics and Stories
doc.snippet: Story breakdown with requirement references to DB §8.2-8.6 for each migration (V2.1-V2.5), detailed task list with subtasks for each schema change, and acceptance criteria.

artifact.reason: Consolidated Flyway migration creating all 5 Phase 2 tables (desktop_threads, desktop_messages, projects, project_members, saved_reports) with full audit support, views, and triggers
Desktop thread entity extending BaseEntity with @Audited annotation, demonstrates audit pattern for future desktop entities. Updated in E1-S2 to align with audit standards.
Base entity providing audit fields (created_by, last_modified_by, created_at, updated_at) inherited by all desktop entities

backend.desc: Moku API - Spring Boot backend (separate repository)

desktop.desc: This project - Electron + Svelte (not directly involved in this story)

constraints.constraint: ALL desktop tables MUST align with Moku/Holo audit standards. Dual ownership tracking required: created_user_id (UUID NOT NULL) for business ownership, created_by/last_modified_by (VARCHAR 255) for system audit. Entities must extend BaseEntity, use @Audited annotation for Hibernate Envers, and @EntityListeners(AuditingEntityListener.class) for Spring Boot auditing.

Use Flyway for versioned migrations. Consolidated V2.1 migration for all Phase 2 tables preferred over separate V2.1-V2.5 migrations for atomic deployment. Migrations must be idempotent where possible.
All migrations must complete in < 5 minutes on databases with up to 100K threads. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid table locks > 30 seconds.

Phase 1 desktop app must continue working after migration (additive changes only). Existing desktop_threads and desktop_messages data must remain intact. New columns use sensible defaults or nullable.
All foreign key constraints must use proper CASCADE/SET NULL behavior. CHECK constraints enforce enum values at database level. Unique indexes prevent data corruption.
Desktop tables (desktop_threads, desktop_messages) use client-generated UUIDs for offline-first architecture. Entities must override BaseEntity.id field to remove @GeneratedValue annotation.
Testing deferred to integration with desktop app. Row-Level Security (RLS) prevents unit tests without full auth flow. Schema verification via end-to-end testing in E1-S2, E1-S3, E1-S4.
Moku API backend is a SEPARATE repository from this desktop project. Migration files located at: moku-api/src/main/resources/db/migration/

interface.name: Flyway Migration API
PostgreSQL JDBC Driver
interface.signature: Connection URL: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/holokai_db Driver class: org.postgresql.Driver Required: PostgreSQL 14+ for JSONB improvements and performance

interface.name: Testcontainers PostgreSQL Module
interface.signature: @Container PostgreSQLContainer postgresqlContainer = new PostgreSQLContainer("postgres:14") Usage: Spin up isolated PostgreSQL instance for migration testing

tests.standards: Use Testcontainers for PostgreSQL integration tests to ensure migrations run in isolated environment. Spring Boot Test with @SpringBootTest for migration testing framework. JUnit 5 for test structure. Test on both clean database and Phase 1 seeded database for backward compatibility. Verify schema matches spec using pg_dump comparison. Test constraint violations by attempting invalid inserts.

ideas.idea: MigrationTest_CleanDatabase: Start Testcontainers PostgreSQL, run V2.1-V2.5 migrations, verify all tables/columns/constraints exist via pg_catalog queries
MigrationTest_Phase1Compatibility: Seed Phase 1 data (users, threads, messages), run V2.1-V2.5, verify data integrity with SELECT queries checking FK validity
MigrationTest_SchemaMatch: After migration, pg_dump schema and compare to database-schema-2025-11-25.md spec (programmatic DDL comparison)
RollbackTest_AllMigrations: Run V2.1-V2.5, then execute rollback scripts in reverse order (V2.5 → V2.1), verify original schema restored
ConstraintTest_CheckConstraints: Attempt invalid inserts (type='invalid', branch_index=3, role='superadmin') and assert constraint violation exceptions
ConstraintTest_ForeignKeyCascade: Insert parent records (projects, threads), delete parents, verify child records cascade deleted or set NULL per spec
IndexTest_AllIndexes: Query pg_indexes WHERE tablename IN ('desktop_threads', 'projects', 'project_members') and assert all expected indexes present
ConstraintTest_UniqueIndexes: Attempt duplicate project_members insert (same project_id, user_id) and duplicate messages (same thread_id, client_message_id), assert unique violation

lesson.title: Audit Architecture Matters Early
lesson.description: Initially created desktop_threads without audit alignment. Later discovered ALL desktop tables lacked audit trail, unlike Moku/Holo entities. Lesson: Verify architecture patterns across all new tables before implementation to avoid rework.
lesson.impact: Significant rework required to align V2.1 migration with audit standards after initial implementation

lesson.title: Dual Ownership Concepts
lesson.description: Desktop tables need TWO ownership concepts: Business ownership (created_user_id UUID - which app user owns/created the entity) and System audit (created_by/last_modified_by VARCHAR - which system user from Spring Security context made changes). Both are critical for compliance and debugging.
lesson.impact: Changed field naming from created_by (UUID) to created_user_id (UUID) to clarify distinction, added created_by (VARCHAR) for system audit

lesson.title: BaseEntity Inheritance with Overrides
lesson.description: DesktopThread successfully extends BaseEntity while overriding id field for client-generated UUIDs. This pattern works for offline-first architecture while maintaining audit capabilities. Override id field and remove @GeneratedValue annotation.
lesson.impact: Enables offline-first desktop architecture while maintaining full audit trail compatibility with Moku/Holo patterns

lesson.title: Consolidated vs Incremental Migrations
lesson.description: Single V2.1 migration (all tables) proved simpler than V2.1-V2.5 (separate migrations). For Phase 2, atomic schema deployment was preferable. Reduces complexity, easier rollback, simpler deployment.
lesson.impact: Changed from planned V2.1-V2.5 separate migrations to single consolidated V2.1 migration containing all 5 tables
